name: verba
description: The Developer's Dictation Extension – Voice dictation with AI-powered post-processing for VS Code

verba.audioDevice.description: Name of the audio input device for recording (e.g. 'Mikrofon (USB MICROPHONE)'). Leave empty for auto-detection. On Windows, run 'ffmpeg -list_devices true -f dshow -i dummy' to see available devices.

verba.terminal.executeCommand.description: If true, sends Enter after inserting dictated text into the terminal (executes the text as a command).

verba.templates.description: Dictation templates that control how the transcribed text is post-processed by Claude.

name.description: Display name shown in the Quick-Pick menu.

prompt.description: System prompt sent to Claude for post-processing the transcript.

contextAware.description: If true, this template triggers semantic code search and includes relevant code snippets as context for Claude.

default.name: Freitext
default.prompt: Clean up the transcript: remove filler words (um, uh, like, you know, halt, eigentlich, sozusagen, quasi), smooth broken or repeated sentence starts, fix obvious transcription errors. Keep the original language and meaning exactly. Return only the cleaned text without explanation.

default.name: Commit Message
default.prompt: Convert this transcript into a Git commit message following Conventional Commits format. First line: type(scope): short description. Optional body after blank line for details. Keep the original language. Return only the commit message without explanation.

default.name: JavaDoc
default.prompt: Convert this transcript into a JavaDoc comment block (/** ... */). Structure with @param, @return, @throws tags as appropriate based on the described function. Keep the original language. Return only the JavaDoc block without explanation.

default.name: Markdown
default.prompt: Convert this transcript into well-structured Markdown text. Use headings, bullet lists, numbered lists, and emphasis as appropriate. Keep the original language. Return only the Markdown without explanation.

default.name: E-Mail
default.prompt: Convert this transcript into a professional email with appropriate greeting and closing. Maintain the original language and intended tone (formal or informal). Return only the email text without explanation.

default.name: Code Comment
default.prompt: Generate a precise code comment based on the transcript and the provided code context. Choose the appropriate format (inline comment, JSDoc, Docstring) based on the programming language. Return ONLY the comment.

default.name: Explain Code
default.prompt: Explain the code based on the question in the transcript and the provided code context. Answer concisely and technically in the language of the transcript. Return ONLY the explanation.

default.name: Claude Code Prompt
default.prompt: Convert this transcript into a precise, well-structured prompt for a Claude Code agent. Use the provided code context to reference specific files, classes, and functions by name. Structure the prompt as a clear task description: what to do, where in the codebase, and any constraints. Keep the original language. Return ONLY the prompt text, ready to paste into a terminal.

default.name: Transform Selection
default.prompt: The user has selected text in their editor (provided in <selection> tags) and dictated an instruction (in <transcript> tags). Apply the spoken instruction to transform the selected text. Examples: translate it, rewrite it, add comments, simplify it, fix it, etc. Return ONLY the transformed text that should replace the selection — no explanation, no preamble.

verba.glossary.description: Terms that must be preserved exactly during transcription and cleanup (e.g. product names, technical terms). These terms are sent as hints to Whisper and as protection instructions to Claude. Recommended limit: ~80 terms (~224 Whisper prompt tokens).

verba.transcription.provider.description: Transcription provider: 'openai' uses the Whisper API (requires API key), 'local' uses whisper.cpp CLI for offline transcription (requires whisper-cpp installed).

verba.transcription.localModel.description: Whisper model to use for local transcription. Larger models are more accurate but slower. Run 'Verba: Download Whisper Model' to download a model.

verba.contextSearch.maxResults.description: Number of context snippets retrieved per dictation (for context-aware templates).

verba.contextSearch.provider.description: Context search provider: 'auto' uses grepai if installed, otherwise OpenAI Embeddings. 'grepai' or 'openai' forces a specific provider.

scripts.test: npm run compile && npm run test:unit && npm run test:integration
